guide.index.title: === Battle Handbook ===
guide.index.topic_battle: battle        Battle system (win rate, type matchup, XP rewards)
guide.index.topic_region: region        Region system (unlock conditions, level ranges, Pokemon pool)
guide.index.topic_xp: xp            XP / leveling (formula, party share, dispatch bonus)
guide.index.topic_item: item          Items (Poke Ball, drop rates, catching)
guide.battle.title: === Battle Handbook ===
guide.battle.encounter_desc1: There is a 15% chance of encountering a wild Pokemon at the start of each session.
guide.battle.encounter_desc2: The party member with the best type matchup will step up to fight automatically.
guide.battle.winrate_formula: Win rate = type matchup x level factor x stat factor
guide.battle.winrate_level_note: steep at low levels, flat at high (Lv.51 vs 55 ~ 37%)
guide.battle.type_grass: Grass -> strong vs Water, Ground, Rock
guide.battle.type_fire: Fire -> strong vs Grass, Ice, Bug, Steel
guide.battle.type_water: Water -> strong vs Fire, Ground, Rock
guide.battle.type_hint: {gray}Full chart: check each Pokemon's type with tokenmon pokedex <name>{reset}
guide.battle.party_desc1: Battle is 1v1, but the whole party's strength adjusts win rate.
guide.battle.party_desc2: Each member's relative power vs the wild Pokemon is calculated -- strongest fights.
guide.battle.party_desc3: Other members contribute a bonus via geometric series weights (r~0.337).
guide.battle.party_formula: {gray}Formula: multiplier = 1 + sum r^i x (score_i / best_score), r ~ 0.337{reset}
guide.battle.reward_win: Win XP = (base 50 + wildLv x 3 + rarity bonus) x multiplier
guide.battle.reward_catch: Catch chance on win = determined by each Pokemon's catch rate
guide.battle.reward_rarity: {gray}Rarity bonus: common=0, uncommon=30, rare=80, legendary=200, mythical=500{reset}
guide.region.title: === Region Handbook ===
guide.region.move_list: tokenmon region list    List all regions
guide.region.move_cmd: tokenmon region move <name>   Move to region
guide.region.tip1: They say higher-level regions have stronger wild Pokemon and more Exp. Points.
guide.region.tip2: Each region has a unique Pokemon pool. Travel far and wide to complete the Pokedex!
guide.region.tip3: Rumor has it Champion Road requires 50 catches... Legendary Pokemon await there.
guide.achievement.title: === Achievement Handbook ===
guide.achievement.check_cmd: tokenmon achievements   Full achievement list with status
guide.achievement.reward_unlock: Pokemon unlock: a new Pokemon is obtained on specific achievements
guide.achievement.reward_xp: XP bonus: permanent XP multiplier increase (stackable)
guide.achievement.reward_ball: Poke Ball: used to catch wild Pokemon
guide.achievement.reward_slot: Party slot: increase max party size
guide.xp.title: === XP / Leveling Handbook ===
guide.xp.gain_desc: Exp. Points are earned on battle wins (0 on loss).
guide.xp.gain_formula: {cyan}XP = (50 + wildLv x 3 + typeDisadvBonus + rarityBonus) x multiplier{reset}
guide.xp.party_desc1: All party members receive the same Exp. Points (not divided -- everyone gets full amount).
guide.xp.party_desc2: Even with 6 party members, each receives the full Exp. Points.
guide.xp.party_tip: {yellow}They say a larger party means training more Pokemon at once!{reset}
guide.xp.dispatch_cmd: tokenmon party dispatch <name>  to set dispatch Pokemon
guide.xp.dispatch_desc: Dispatch Pokemon receives 1.5x XP during subagent runs.
guide.xp.dispatch_tip: {yellow}They say dispatching lower-level Pokemon helps them grow faster!{reset}
guide.xp.expgroup_desc1: Each Pokemon has a different exp group (fast, medium_fast, medium_slow, slow, etc.)
guide.xp.expgroup_desc2: Required XP can differ even at the same level.
guide.item.title: === Item Handbook ===
guide.item.pokeball_desc: A consumable item used to catch wild Pokemon.
guide.item.obtain_achievement: Achievement rewards (first win: 3, Pokedex researcher: 5, win streak master: 10, etc.)
guide.item.catch_desc1: Battle win + uncaught Pokemon + hold Poke Ball -> catch (uses 1 ball)
guide.item.catch_desc2: No ball = no catch even on win (Pokedex registered + XP only).
guide.item.catch_desc3: No ball consumed when battling already-caught Pokemon.
guide.item.catch_tip: {yellow}They say it's wise to stock up on Poke Balls before venturing into new areas!{reset}

region.locked: This region unlocks after catching/seeing {count} {label} Pokemon.
hook.evolution_block_reason:
What?! Party pokemon are trying to evolve!

You MUST call AskUserQuestion. One subquestion per pokemon (batch up to 4 pokemon per call).

Per-subquestion rules:
- `question` field: copy the candidate's 'Use this question VERBATIM' line EXACTLY — do not paraphrase, do not reformat.
- `options` (buttons, max 4):
  - 3 or fewer targets → all targets + 'Refuse'.
  - 4+ targets → first 3 targets + 'Refuse'. List the remaining targets on a separate line appended to the question body as 'Other forms: A, B, C' so the Trainer can type one via 'Other'.
- `multiSelect`: false

Candidates:
{candidateList}

Handling the Trainer's answer:
- Button selecting a target → run `tokenmon evolve <pokemon> <target>`.
- 'Refuse' button or Other with 'refuse'/'no'/'cancel' → do nothing (no auto re-prompt).
- For free-text 'Other' input, VALIDATE before running `tokenmon evolve`:
  - If the input (case-insensitive, accepting localized or English names) matches a pokemon name in the 'All evolution targets' list, evolve to that target.
  - If it doesn't match, reply with something like "I didn't recognize that — please pick a button or type one of: {targets}, refuse" and re-invoke the same AskUserQuestion (cap at 2 re-prompts to avoid loops; after that, treat as 'Refuse').
tier.heated: The tall grass is rustling intensely... (Next: encounter 1.5x, XP 1.5x)
tier.intense: Something seems to be lurking nearby... (Next: encounter 2.5x, XP 2.5x)
tier.legendary: The air is crackling with powerful energy! (Next: encounter 4x, XP 5x)
tip.volume_xp: They say using more tokens at once yields more Exp. Points.
tip.volume_encounter: Rumor has it wild Pokemon appear more often during longer tasks.
tip.volume_rare: They say stronger Pokemon are drawn to complex tasks...
tip.battle_type_advantage: They say type matchups greatly affect win rate. Target weaknesses!
tip.battle_level_matters: Large level gaps sharply shift win rate (Lv.4 vs Lv.12 ~ 17%). Train hard!
tip.battle_best_pokemon: The party member with the best type matchup steps up to fight automatically.
tip.battle_xp_loss: Losing a battle earns no Exp. Points. Keep strong Pokemon in the party!
tip.battle_ball_loss: Losing to strong wild Pokemon will cost you Poké Balls... Don't push your luck!
tip.battle_rare_bonus: They say rarer wild Pokemon give bigger XP bonuses on victory.
tip.region_next_unlock: Discover {remaining} more Pokemon to unlock the next region!
tip.region_current_level: Current region level range: Lv.{minLv}~{maxLv}. Match your party levels.
tip.region_higher_xp: They say higher-level regions have stronger wild Pokemon and more Exp. Points.
tip.region_champion_road: Rumor has it Champion Road unlocks at 50 catches... Legendary Pokemon await.
tip.region_pool_size: Each region has a unique Pokemon pool. Travel far and wide to complete the Pokedex!
tip.achievement_rewards: Achievement rewards include Pokemon unlocks, XP bonuses, and party slot expansions.
tip.achievement_session_count: They say special rewards await at 10, 50, and 100 sessions.
tip.achievement_token_milestones: Rumor has it Pokemon unlock at 100k, 500k, 1M, 2M, and 5M token milestones!
tip.achievement_pokedex_master: They say catching 100 species (Pokedex Master) unlocks the mythical Arceus!
tip.xp_party_share: All party members receive the same Exp. Points. A bigger party trains more Pokemon at once!
tip.xp_dispatch_bonus: Dispatched Pokemon earn 1.5x XP from subagent runs!
tip.xp_achievement_bonus: They say permanent XP bonuses can be unlocked through achievements (up to +55%).
tip.xp_weakest_member: They say dispatching the weakest party member {pokemon} (Lv.{level}) helps it grow faster.
tip.xp_evolution: If a Pokemon is close to evolving, focus Exp. Points on it!
tip.item_tool_drop: 🔴 Using tools sometimes lets you find Poké Balls!
tip.item_subagent_drop: 🔴 Subagents always bring back Poké Balls!
tip.item_session_bonus: 🔴 You get bonus Poké Balls when a session ends!
tip.item_field_drop: 🔴 Sometimes Poké Balls are just lying around in the field!
tip.item_ball_drop: 🔴 Poké Balls drop at 30% on victory, 12% on loss
tip.item_ball_catch: You can't catch wild Pokemon without a Poke Ball. Always keep spares!
tip.item_ball_count: Current Poke Balls: {count}. Stock up for new encounters!
tip.item_achievement_balls: They say achievement rewards can give large batches of Poke Balls (up to 10!).
tip.item_ball_strategy: Battling already-caught Pokemon does not consume a Poke Ball.
item_drop.tool: You found a Poké Ball during your adventure! You obtained {n} Poké Ball(s)!
item_drop.subagent: Your ally brought back Poké Balls! You obtained {n} Poké Ball(s)!
item_drop.session_end: You received {n} Poké Ball(s) as today's adventure reward!

rewards.type_master_legendary: ✨ {count} types mastered! Special legendary group unlocked!
